subject: Social Science
chapter_name: Social and Political Life – II: Understanding Media
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze media bias, perspectives, and framing
Investigate misinformation and its societal impact
Develop ethical understanding of media responsibilities
Plan and present media literacy campaigns
Enhance research, critical thinking, and multimedia communication skills

lesson_plan.title: Critical Media Analysis
teaching_strategy: Provide multiple articles on the same event
Guide students to compare reporting styles and identify perspectives
student_activities: Prepare a short report identifying bias and objective facts

lesson_plan.title: Investigating Misinformation
student_activities: Select a trending online post or news
Verify its authenticity and document findings

lesson_plan.title: Media Ethics and Responsibilities
teaching_strategy: Present case studies on ethical challenges in reporting
Class discussion on possible ethical solutions

lesson_plan.title: Multimedia Media Awareness Campaign
topics_covered: Designing posters, videos, and digital content
teaching_strategy: Guide students in planning message, visuals, and medium
student_activities: Design a short multimedia campaign on verifying news
Present or record it for the class
assessment: Creativity, clarity, and effectiveness of campaign

lesson_plan.title: Investigative Journalism Mini-Project
teaching_strategy: Guide students in collecting data and structuring a report
Provide checklists for verification and balanced reporting
student_activities: Identify a local issue or event
Collect information and write a detailed news report

lesson_plan.title: Media Impact Reflection and Debate
topics_covered: Social and cultural impact of news and advertisements
teaching_strategy: Class debate or discussion on media influence
Use examples from social media, TV, and newspapers
student_activities: Prepare arguments for debate on media impact
Write a reflection on their own media use and responsibility

teaching_aids: Newspaper articles, online news portals, and social media examples
Charts or slides explaining media bias and ethics
Tools for creating posters, videos, and presentations
evaluation_methods: Observation of critical thinking and research skills
teacher_tip: Encourage students to question information critically, compare sources, and think ethically about media. Use real-world examples and guide them in creating projects and campaigns for active learning.
